lesson: A LOW MIX alarm with a full hopper, a clear feed line and a sensor reading open-circuit is a dead level probe. Its sudden onset is the tell — scale chokes a line gradually.

tests.error-log: LOW MIX alarm came on overnight and never cleared, no matter what the staff topped up.
tests.inspect-beater: Mix line is clear and flowing freely. Nothing blocking the feed.
tests.continuity-test: Mix level sensor reads open-circuit regardless of level. It's dead.

flavour: The hopper was full. The sensor was full of it.
authenticityNote: Level probes fail open and report empty forever. Same alarm as a scale-blocked line, but sudden onset and a clear feed line — and the continuity test settles it outright.
